one_liner: BigCommerce offers broad, self-serve REST and GraphQL Admin/Storefront APIs authenticated via OAuth-issued access tokens, with an official MCP server already available.

auth_methods.notes: Store-level or app-level API accounts issue OAuth access tokens used via X-Auth-Token header for REST/GraphQL Admin APIs.
GraphQL Storefront API uses Authorization: Bearer token; B2B APIs use X-Store-Hash plus X-Auth-Token.

self_serve_notes: No free production tier, but a 15-day free trial store allows creating API accounts and access tokens self-serve via Settings > API Accounts in the control panel. Paid plans required for production use, with rate limits varying by tier (20k-60k req/hr).

api_surface.notes: Broad REST Admin APIs (Catalog, Content, Management, Webhooks, Storefront Token Management) plus GraphQL Admin API and GraphQL Storefront API.

evidence.supports: OAuth access tokens via X-Auth-Token header for REST/GraphQL Admin APIs; API accounts required.
Broad REST Admin API surface (Catalog, Content, Management, Webhooks) authenticated via OAuth store-level API accounts.
GraphQL Storefront API uses Authorization Bearer token.
No free production tier; paid plans required, 15-day free trial available for development/testing.

flags: Official BigCommerce MCP server exists (docs.bigcommerce.com/_mcp/server) plus a separate Storefront MCP in Early Access and multiple community MCP servers.
Self-serve credential creation requires either a paid plan or 15-day trial store; no permanent free tier for production use.
